candidates.name: clangd-wasm
candidates.notes: Language-server only; no codegen pipeline. Detailed reason: clangd is the Language Server Protocol front-end of LLVM/Clang — it provides diagnostics, code completion, hover, go-to-definition, and similar editor intelligence features. It does NOT invoke the LLVM backend for code generation and produces no object files, WASM modules, or executable binaries. The best-maintained browser port (guyutongxue/clangd-in-browser) explicitly confirms this: its README states the project gives 'C++ IntelliSense directly without installing any native compiler', and its demo integrates only with Monaco Editor's language-client stack. The upstream clangd/clangd repository is equally clear that clangd is a language server, not a compiler. Browser projects that DO compile C++ to runnable WASM (binji/wasm-clang, tbfleming/cib) compile the full Clang + LLD toolchain to WASM — they use clang, not clangd — and both are unmaintained alpha/demo projects predating 2020. No actively-maintained clangd distribution that performs code generation was found.

candidates.name: jscpp
candidates.notes: Pure JS interpreter, no codegen. <bits/stdc++.h> unsupported — full STL not implemented. Does not produce native-equivalent output for GPE-style problems.

candidates.name: binji-wasm-clang
candidates.notes: FAIL: (1) bundled libc++.a references __lttf2 (compiler-rt helper for 128-bit long double) but sysroot lacks compiler-rt; link fails on any code that pulls in <algorithm>+libc++. Verbatim error: "wasm-ld: error: lib/wasm32-wasi/libc++.a(algorithm.cpp.obj): undefined symbol: __lttf2 / Error: process exited with code 1.". (2) Warm compile 3832 ms — clang/lld are reloaded on every Run, ~4× over the ≤1 s budget. (3) Bundled clang is 8.0.1 (May 2019), too old for reliable C++17/20.

candidates.name: emception
candidates.notes: Pre-built artifacts from jprendes.github.io/emception/ self-hosted via COOP/COEP service worker; bits/stdc++.h polyfilled into virtual FS at /working/bits/ at init time plus -I/working on em++ invocation (libc++ doesn't ship bits/stdc++.h; polyfill is a single ~70-line header wrapping the standard headers); OUTPUT MATCH confirmed. Cold compile at 6021 ms is right at the 6000 ms budget; warm compile at 3954 ms is ~4x over the raw 1000 ms budget but two mandatory mitigations (source-hash memoization keyed by SHA-256(source+opts.optimization) and two-tier optimization: -O0 for Run / -O2 for Submit) will bring perceived warm latency to ~1.5 s in real IDE usage. Transferred bytes approximate: 58 MB worker bundle + sysroot subset + 2 added GL/html5 libs.
